heading: Accessible recreation inventory
label: 8481 Accessible recreation inventory
notes: References in TextThe Architectural Barriers Act of 1968, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1) and (c), is , , , which is classified generally to chapter 51 (§ 4151 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 90–48082 Stat. 718section 4151 of Title 42Aug. 12, 1968, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1) and (c), was in the original a reference to section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act, and was translated as meaning section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, , to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Section 794 of title 29Pub. children.label: A
children.text: camp shelters, camping facilities, and camping units;

children.label: C
children.text: hunting, fishing, shooting, or archery ranges or locations;

children.label: E
children.text: picnic facilities and picnic units; and

children.label: F
children.text: any other outdoor recreation facilities, as determined by the Secretary concerned; and

children.label: A
children.text: each of the Federal land management agencies; and

children.label: B
children.text: each relevant unit and subunit of the Federal land management agencies.

children.heading: Inclusion of current assessments
children.label: b Inclusion of current assessments
children.text: As part of the comprehensive assessment required under subsection (a)(1), to the extent practicable, the Secretary concerned may rely on assessments completed or data gathered prior to .January 4, 2025

children.heading: Public information
children.label: c Public information
children.text: Not later than 7 years after , the Secretary concerned shall identify opportunities to create, update, or replace signage and other publicly available information, including web page information, related to accessibility and consistent with the Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 ( et seq.) and at outdoor recreation facilities covered by the assessment required under subsection (a)(1).January 4, 202542 U.S.C. 4151section 794 of title 29
